parameterGroups.label: Reverb Character
parameterGroups.rationale: RoomSize and Damping define the reverb tail behaviour

parameters.name: Room Size
parameters.description: Perceived room size from 0 (small) to 1 (large cathedral)

parameters.name: Damping
parameters.description: High frequency absorption where higher values create a darker reverb tail

parameterGroups.label: Mix
parameterGroups.rationale: Separate wet and dry level controls rather than a single mix knob

parameters.name: Wet Level
parameters.description: Wet/dry mix where 0 is fully dry and 1 is fully wet

parameters.name: Dry Level
parameters.description: Level of the dry signal mixed into the output

parameterGroups.label: Stereo
parameterGroups.rationale: Width controls the stereo spread of the reverb output

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: Stereo width of the reverb where 0 is mono and 1 is full stereo

parameterGroups.label: Special
parameterGroups.rationale: FreezeMode sustains the reverb tail indefinitely

parameters.name: Freeze Mode
parameters.description: Freeze mode that sustains the reverb tail indefinitely

gaps.question: What is the processing order? Does it use the JUCE Reverb class (Freeverb algorithm) directly, or a custom implementation? What is the internal structure (comb filters, allpass filters)?
gaps.context: Need to understand the reverb topology at a high level for the pseudo-code.

gaps.question: FreezeMode has a continuous range 0-1 with default 0.1, not a toggle. How does this parameter actually work? Is it a threshold (above X = frozen), or does it continuously affect the feedback amount?
gaps.context: The continuous range is unusual for a 'freeze' function - users need to understand how it works.

gaps.question: WetLevel and DryLevel are independent. Are they simple linear gain multipliers? Can both be set to 1.0 (boosted output)?
gaps.context: Separate wet/dry controls behave differently from a single mix knob.

gaps.question: How is the Width parameter implemented? Mid/side processing, or decorrelated left/right outputs from the algorithm?
gaps.context: Affects how users understand the stereo behaviour.

gaps.question: What is the internal complexity? Number of comb/allpass filters? Per-sample processing?

notes: SimpleReverb is based on the Freeverb algorithm (per the description). It has separate WetLevel and DryLevel controls rather than a single Mix knob. FreezeMode has a continuous range which is unusual. Known scriptnode equivalent: fx.reverb.
